title: Drop In Centers * Sexual Assault Issues - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Housing & Shelter > Drop In Centers * Sexual Assault Issues 3 Matching Service Providers Refine Results - Select - Sexual Assault Issues p In Centers Sexual Assault Issues Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF SEXUAL ASSAULT SUPPORT SERVICES OF LANE COUNTY SEXUAL ASSAULT SUPPORT SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides support for survivors of past and present sexual assault, abuse, and harassment and for friends and family of survivors. Services include a crisis line, medical response and legal advocacy, support groups, peer support, community education and outreach for prevention, and information and referral. All services are confidential. 1902 Jefferson Street Suite 1 Eugene, OR 97405 Eligibility: Survivors of sexual assault, abuse, and harassment, and their friends and family members Hours: -Crisis line, advocacy, legal, and medical response: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week -All other services: Hours vary (844) 404-7700 Get Directions Visit Website More Details THE HARBOR Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers a day center, domestic and sexual violence advocacy, culturally specific LGBTQ2+ and Latinx services, relocation assistance, transportation to a safe location, accompaniment to medical or forensic exams, assistance applying for Crime Victims Compensation, and information and referral to community agencies. Provides educational programs. These programs focus on raising awareness about the dynamics of domestic and sexual violence, understanding trauma, and promoting prevention strategies within the community. Currently accepting volunteers. 801 Commercial Street Astoria, OR 97103 Eligibility: Age 14 and older Hours: Advocacy Office: Monday/Tuesday/Thursday/Friday 9am-4pm, Wednesday 9am-noon, closed 1st Wednesday of every month. Online Chat and Text: Monday-Friday 10am-5pm Email (503) 325-5735 Get Directions Visit Website More Details TIDES OF CHANGE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a domestic violence and sexual assault helpline and resource drop-in center. Services include counseling, support groups, advocacy, transportation, health advocacy, rental assistance, accompaniment services, protection order assistance, 24-hour crisis intervention, limited financial assistance, and a hotline. 1902 2nd Street Tillamook, OR 97141 Eligibility: Individuals who are the victims of domestic violence, sexual assault, stalking, trafficking, or dating violence Hours: Helpline: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Office: Monday/Tuesday/Thursday/Friday 10am-4pm, Wednesday noon-4pm Email (503) 842-9486 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SEXUAL ASSAULT SUPPORT SERVICES OF LANE COUNTY SEXUAL ASSAULT SUPPORT SERVICES THE HARBOR THE HARBOR TIDES OF CHANGE TIDES OF CHANGE
